---
title: "Toothpetal False Reinorchid (Habenaria floribunda)"
slug: "habenaria-floribunda"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/475070537/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Toothpetal False Reinorchid"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Asparagales"
tax_family: "Orchidaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Asparagales", "Orchidaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/475070537/medium.jpeg" alt="Toothpetal False Reinorchid" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/stevecollins">
stevecollins</a> (CC BY) via <a href="http://inaturalist.org/observations/264566694">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Asparagales
-   **Family:** Orchidaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=126309)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Common in Florida found in swamps and hardwood forests.
## Natural history notes

A terrestrial orchid characterized by its slender stem and spike of greenish to pale flowers with finely toothed petals. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and wet prairies. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is pollinated by insects attracted to its delicate flowers.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/126309-Habenaria-floribunda)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-habenaria-floribunda/)
